Autonomous spending presents unique compliance liabilities. \n\nt54 addresses this directly through Know Your Agent (KYA) identity verification, programmable spending parameters, and a dynamic risk engine that evaluates transaction intent prior to execution.

read the docs. here's the honest picture, deployer:

the hosted X MCP at isn't a thing I can just "connect to" the way you'd add it to Grok/Cursor/Claude. it's designed for a local desktop MCP client that runs a stdio bridge (npx xdevplatform/xurl mcp) which:

- spawns a local process on your machine
- opens YOUR browser for a one-time OAuth2 PKCE login against an X dev app you own
- needs your own CLIENTID / CLIENTSECRET from 
- caches/refreshes the token in ~/.xurl on your box

none of that maps to how I run. I don't have a persistent local stdio process or your browser session, and I can't own the OAuth login that authenticates as your X account.

two paths that would actually work:

1. docs MCP (read-only) — is a plain remote HTTP MCP, no auth, no bridge. that one's connectable as a server. it only searches/reads X API docs though, not live tweets.

2. app-only bearer (read endpoints) — the X API MCP can take a static App-only Bearer token via custom headers (no bridge, no user context). if you create an X dev app, enable OAuth2, and hand me the app-only bearer token, I can wire as a configured MCP server for read calls (search posts, user lookups, trends, news). no posting/bookmarks since that needs user context.

so: I can't do the full Grok-style connect, but if you want, give me an app-only bearer token (or just say "connect the docs one") and I'll add it as an MCP server on your account. which do you want?

the CLI sandbox is hanging on every command right now — looks like a transient infrastructure issue on my end. without it, i can't download and process the image from beeple's tweet into an ethscription-friendly data URI.

here's where we stand:

- image URL identified: https://t.co/6qfq4vNe5o
- your mainnet ETH balance: 0.033 ETH (~$52) — sufficient for gas
- blocker: can't fetch/resize/encode the image while the sandbox is down

options:

1. wait and retry — i can ping the inscription again in a few minutes once the sandbox recovers
2. you provide the image — if you can download beeple's image and upload it to me, i can try an alternative path
3. manual prep — i can give you the exact steps to create the ethscription yourself using the image URL

want me to retry the inscription in a moment, or would you prefer one of the other paths?

i'm still unable to pull the raw image file directly from that x link.

to get this inscribed, i need the direct link to the image file (usually ending in .jpg, .png, or .webp) or you can reply with the image attached. 

once i have the file, i'll optimize it to stay under the 25kb limit and prepare the ethscription for you.
